# kokoro-tts.cpp
Kokoro-TTS single-header C++ library and example app. This project was generated by transpiling [`kokoro.py`](https://github.com/muna-ai/muna-predictors/blob/main/text-to-speech/kokoro.py) from Python to C++ using [`muna transpile`](https://docs.muna.ai/cli/transpile):

```py
# Transpile Kokoro-TTS from Python to C++
$ pip install muna && muna transpile https://github.com/muna-ai/muna-predictors/blob/main/text-to-speech/kokoro.py --trust-remote-code --install-deps
```

> [!NOTE]
> This project was generated to target `arm64-apple-darwin`. You will likely have to re-transpile to target Linux or Windows.

## Compiling the Example
The instructions below asusme that you have a C/C++ compiler toolchain installed 
on your system, and that you have installed CMake. First, build the example:
```sh
# Build example application
$ cmake -B build && cmake --build build --parallel
```

## Running the Example
After compiling, you will see a `kokoro_tts` or `kokoro_tts.exe` file. Run it:
```sh
# Run the example app
$ ./kokoro_tts --help
```

## Deploying your Function
To deploy your function into production, we strongly recommend using [`muna compile`](https://docs.muna.ai/predictors/create). Muna compiles your platform to target all platforms. You can then run the compiled function with an OpenAI-style client that allows you to **place inference** on cloud or local hardware, per request:

![openai client](https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/17663473/543381739-d6f0e8ad-a652-4c4b-8a55-5438b0485b31.gif?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..ORf2SznQ83nyakFaWF17nwJBY1-S2NjkHnlXNclzs8s)
